description_long: This is a cylindrical stone tower with a conical, dark gray roof, reminiscent of medieval fortifications. The exterior is constructed from large, textured stone blocks, with two prominent horizontal bands encircling the structure at the top and bottom of the main shaft. Small, arched windows with metal grilles are evenly spaced around the upper and lower sections, providing a fortified appearance. The roof is steeply pitched and appears to be made of slate or similar dark material, giving the tower a weathered, historic look. There are no visible doors or entrances, and the base is flush with the ground, suggesting a defensive or watchtower function. The overall design is simple, robust, and utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements.
description: A cylindrical stone tower with a conical dark roof and small arched windows, resembling a medieval watchtower. The structure is robust and utilitarian, with stone block walls and minimal decoration.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical stone body, conical roof, and two arched windows, one on each level.
description_view_1: This side view reveals three arched windows, two on the upper level and one on the lower, with the stone texture and roof clearly visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays two arched windows, one on each level, and the stonework bands encircling the tower.
description_view_3: This view shows three arched windows, two on the upper level and one on the lower, with lighter stone coloration visible.
